Title: Story 01.2: Enhanced Step Navigator
Description:
Add visual step indicators showing user's progress through the 5-phase innovation journey, with tooltips showing phase details and ability to jump to previous steps.

Where it appears: Top of each phase page (Challenge, Market, Ideation, Investment, Pitch)

What to show:

  • 5 horizontal steps with icons: Challenge → Market → Ideation → Investment → Pitch
  • Current step highlighted, completed steps with checkmarks, future steps grayed out
  • Hover tooltip showing step name and brief description
  • Skip-to dropdown for returning users to jump between completed phases
  • Progress percentage indicator

Component: Enhance existing components/wizard/progress-tracker.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria:
• 5-step navigator appears at top of all phase pages
  • Completed steps show green checkmark icon
  • Current step highlighted with accent color
  • Hover shows tooltip with phase name and description
  • Dropdown allows jumping to any completed phase
  • Progress bar shows overall completion percentage

Title: Story 01.1: Real-time Idea Validation
Description:
Add visual feedback to form inputs showing character limits, validation status, and completion progress for better user experience during idea submission.

Where it appears: Challenge page form fields (problem description, target audience, current solutions)

What to show:

  • Character counter below each textarea (e.g., "50/200")
  • Green checkmark icon when field is valid and complete
  • Red border and error message when validation fails
  • Progress indicator showing form completion %
  • Real-time validation as user types (not just on blur/submit)

Component: Enhance components/ui/input.tsx and components/ui/textarea.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Character counter appears below textareas (e.g., "50/200 chars")
  • Green checkmark shows when field meets minimum requirements
  • Red outline and error message for invalid input
  • Overall form progress bar shows completion percentage
  • Validation happens in real-time as user types

Title: Story 10.2: Micro-Interactions and Animations

Add subtle animations and micro-interactions throughout the UI for better feedback and polished feel.

Where it appears: Throughout the app (buttons, cards, forms, page transitions)

What to add:

  • Button hover/active states with scale/color transitions

  • Success/error toast animations (slide in + fade)
  • Smooth page transitions between wizard steps
  • Card hover effect (slight elevation + shadow increase)
  • Form field focus ring animation
  • Progress bar fill animation

AcceptanceCriteria:
• Buttons have smooth hover (scale 1.02) and active (scale 0.98) transitions
  • Loading skeleton shows shimmer animation during data fetch
  • Toast messages slide in from right with fade effect
  • Page transitions have smooth fade between phases
  • Cards elevate slightly (shadow increase) on hover
  • Form inputs show animated focus ring
  • Progress bars animate from 0 to actual value

Title: Story 10.3: Loading States and Empty States
Description:
Create reusable loading and empty state components for consistent feedback when content is loading or unavailable.

Where it appears: Throughout app (idea list, dashboard, any async content area)

What to show:

  • Loading: Spinner icon or skeleton screens
  • Empty: Illustration, helpful message, call-to-action button
  • Error: Error icon, message explaining issue, retry button

Mock examples:

  • Loading ideas: 3 skeleton cards with shimmer
  • No ideas: Lightbulb icon + "No ideas yet. Click Generate Ideas to start."
  • Error: Alert triangle + "Failed to load. Please try again." + Retry button

Component: Create components/ui/loading-skeleton.tsx, components/ui/empty-state.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Loading skeleton shows 3 placeholder cards with shimmer animation
  • Empty state shows icon + message + CTA button
  • Error state shows icon + message + retry button
  • Components accept props for custom message and action
  • Used consistently across all list views and data displays

Title: Story 10.4: Responsive Design Optimization
Description:
Optimize the application for mobile devices with responsive navigation, touch-friendly targets, and adaptive layouts.

Where it appears: All pages on mobile (<768px)

What to optimize:

  • Hamburger menu for navigation (replaces desktop sidebar)
  • Bottom navigation bar for quick phase switching
  • All tap targets minimum 44x44px
  • Adaptive typography (smaller base font on mobile)
  • Stack cards vertically (no side-by-side on mobile)
  • Responsive charts (simplified view on mobile)
  • Chat panel becomes full screen on mobile

Component: Update components/wizard/phase-layout.tsx, components/ui/navigation.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Mobile shows hamburger menu in header
  • Bottom nav bar with 5 phase icons for quick switching
  • All buttons and tappable areas are minimum 44x44px
  • Cards stack vertically (no horizontal scrolling)
  • Charts switch to simplified mobile view
  • Chat panel expands to full width on mobile
  • No horizontal scroll on any page

Title: Story 10.8: Error Handling and Validation UX

What to add:

  • Warning state for risky actions (yellow border + icon)
  • Confirmation dialog before destructive actions (delete, clear, reset)
  • Toast notifications for success/error feedback

Component: Enhance components/ui/form.tsx, components/ui/toast.tsx
AcceptanceCriteria:
• Inline error messages appear below invalid fields
  • Error messages are specific and actionable
  • Warning state shows yellow border with icon for risky actions
  • Confirmation dialog appears before delete/clear operations
  • Toast notifications show success/error messages
  • Form-level error summary at top when submitting with errors

  • Each item: ID (INV-2024-012), title, 1-line summary, stage badge
  • Color-coded: Red >80%, Yellow 60-80%, Green <60%

AcceptanceCriteria:
• Similar ideas appear in chat after scoring

  • Each idea shows: ID, title, summary, stage, similarity %
  • Color-coded badges with warning for >80%
  • Expandable card or modal for full details
  • Uses static mock data (no API)
